Industries| Energy
Mitsubishi Mulls Options for Japan Offshore Wind Including
Exit
By Shoko Oda
August 27, 2025 at 7:06 AM GMT+5:30
Mitsubishi Corp. is considering all options for three Japanese oﬀshore wind projects,
including an exit, after the company initiated a review earlier this year following
changes to the economic outlook. No decision has been made at this point, the company said in a statement on
Wednesday in response to media reports that Mitsubishi was in the process of
withdrawing from the wind projects. The trading house didn’t elaborate further on
what other options it was considering. “The reports highlight the diﬃculty of making oﬀshore wind projects proﬁtable in
Japan, ” Syusaku Nishikawa, an analyst at Daiwa Securities Co., wrote in a research
note.

“The reports highlight the diﬃculty of making oﬀshore wind projects proﬁtable in
Japan, ” Syusaku Nishikawa, an analyst at Daiwa Securities Co., wrote in a research
note. Still, “there is some room for an optimistic view, namely companies may be able
to win projects if sites are up for re-bidding, or if proﬁtability of future projects can be
improved. ”
Rising costs have upended investment plans in the oﬀshore wind sector across the
world, including higher interest rates and tight supply chains, while some nations are
backing away from green policies — most notably the US. President Donald Trump’s
stance against wind has derailed several projects. Read More: Trump Channels Hatred for Wind Farms Into Strike Against Orsted
Mitsubishi, along with a unit of Chubu Electric Power Co.

Read More: Trump Channels Hatred for Wind Farms Into Strike Against Orsted
Mitsubishi, along with a unit of Chubu Electric Power Co. and Venti Japan Inc., won
the rights to develop the three projects in 2021. Contact us:
